stemHtml: During asymmetric flight, if the wings are kept level using ailerons, a side force on the fuselage is the result of:

options.a: Sideslip due to the rudder force away from the live engine.
options.b: Skid due to the rudder force towards the live engine.
options.c: Sideslip due to the rudder force away from the live engine. This force acts in the same direction of the fuselage side force.
options.d: Skid due to yaw from the live engine. This force acts in the same direction as the rudder force.
